steps.title: Substitute the dog count
steps.content:
The equation uses $d$ for the number of dogs. Since the business cares for $32$ dogs, substitute $d=32$ into the equation.

$$2.5c+4(32)=128$$

steps.title: Solve for cats
steps.content: Simplify the dog term, then isolate $c$. $$2.5c+128=128$$ $$2.5c=0$$ $$c=0$$ The answer is C.
